answer: The system uses Argon2id for password hashing with parameters: 64MB memory, 3 iterations, and a parallelism factor of 4 (64M/3/4).
The refresh token has an expiration period of 7 days and follows a rotation policy (7d-rotate).
The Notification Service runs on port 9200.
The database is currently running RDS-PG 15.4, having been upgraded from version 14.9.
The API Gateway throttle limits are 5000 requests for burst and 2000 requests per second for steady-state.
Argon2id was chosen over bcrypt to mitigate side-channel attacks.
Drizzle is used instead of Prisma to achieve a zero-dependency architecture (zero-deps).
They verify auth tokens using RS256 via a JWKS endpoint, supported by an LRU cache (1000 entries/5 min) for resilience.
Rate limiting is implemented at the ALB to provide DDoS protection before traffic reaches the application containers.
The chain involves the API Gateway (schema validation), a Lambda for JWKS claims, VPC Link, the ALB, and finally the Portfolio Service which applies the RBAC role hierarchy.
If JWKS goes down, all services fail (all-fail). However, an LRU cache (1000/5min) provides a grace period, and token issuance continues if the private key is local.
Real-time alerting depends on WebSockets (planned for Q3) and is currently impacted by an investigation into unbounded memory usage in Celery.
Yes, it was fixed by transitioning to unix timestamps for expiration logic (tz-UTC13-14).
Implementation would leverage the existing webhook system which supports exponential backoff with 5 attempts over 24 hours.
With a connection pool size of 20 per instance, 5 instances would allow a maximum of 100 simultaneous database connections.
While the connection pool limits DB pressure (pool size 20), tax-lot processing is currently facing performance issues due to 'unbounded memory' in Celery workers.
No, Fargate Spot is strictly reserved for batch processing (Spot-ONLY-batch) and not for critical services like Auth.
The requirement for blue-green deployments (zero-downtime) and stateless JWT-based authentication prohibits the use of local in-memory session caches.
The 30-day backups support the disaster recovery targets of a 1-hour Recovery Point Objective (RPO) and a 4-hour Recovery Time Objective (RTO) in us-west-2.
The upgrade required updating connection strings to use 'sslmode=verify-full' and a 'global-bundle' for certificate validation.
The columns are id, account_id, security_id, acquisition_date, cost_basis, quantity, adjusted, and created_at.
MFA adds a TOTP verification step after password validation (handlePostLogin) but before the access and refresh tokens are issued.
The system generates 10 single-use recovery codes.
The policy is exponential backoff with 5 attempts over a 24-hour window.
At rest, PII is encrypted with AES-256-GCM per tenant and passwords use Argon2id. In transit, Envoy mTLS and RDS sslmode=verify-full are mandated.
Lifecycle: Gateway (schema/claims) -> VPC Link -> ALB (IP limit 1000/min) -> Auth handlePostLogin (Argon2id) -> MFA-TOTP -> Issuance of tokens.
The service must use the handle{Verb}{Resource} naming, RFC 7807 error formats, JSON logging, testcontainers for testing, and adhere to mTLS/JWKS constraints.
The API Gateway allows the request (limit 2000/s), but the ALB layer triggers a rate limit (1000/min/IP), throttling 500 of the requests.
The Auth Service (for signing), the API Gateway Lambda (for claims injection), and all downstream services like Portfolio and Notify (for JWKS verification).
GHA (lint/tc/test/build) -> ECR -> ECS Fargate (us-east-1) -> ClaudeCode-review -> Blue-green deployment via ALB for zero downtime.
